message:
🐛 Fix trivy version in nightly workflow (#2525)

* 🐛 Make nightly install step resilient to individual failures

Switch from 'bash -e' to 'bash {0}' (no -e flag) for the install step.
With bash -e, any single tool failure (even transient wget errors) aborts
the entire step and prevents all 32 test suites from running.

- Each tool install is now isolated with its own error handling
- Failures emit GitHub Actions warnings instead of hard errors
- Tool verification summary printed at the end
- Removed wget -q flag for better diagnostics
- Test scripts already handle missing tools gracefully

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix trivy version — v0.58.1 release was removed from GitHub

Trivy v0.58.1 no longer exists on GitHub Releases (404), causing the
container-scan-test to fail. Update to v0.69.3 (latest stable).

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -56,7 +56,7 @@ jobs:
         env:
           GOSEC_VERSION: '2.21.4'
           GITLEAKS_VERSION: '8.21.2'
-          TRIVY_VERSION: '0.58.1'
+          TRIVY_VERSION: '0.69.3'
           SEMGREP_VERSION: '1.102.0'
         shell: bash {0}
         run: |
